item.description: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help lure LeBron from Los Angeles to the Bay Area once free agency officially opens on June 30. The pitch would be simple: reunite with AD, team up with Steph Curry and Draymond Green, play for Steve Kerr, and chase one more championship with a roster of legends.

item.title: Warriors head coach Steve Kerr met with Robinson after …
item.description: Now as he enters his 11th season with the Warriors, Robinson is about to step into a new role as the team’s NBA Summer League head coach. Warriors head coach Steve Kerr met with Robinson after the season ended and told him the news, and that he thought manning the sidelines throughout summer league would be a great opportunity for Robinson's personal and professional career development as the next challenge. Naming Khalid Robinson as the Warriors’ summer league head coach falls in line with how

item.title: The Golden State Warriors have tendered a qualifying …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Golden State Warriors have tendered a qualifying offer to Quinten Post and two-way qualifying offers to Taran Armstrong and Nico Mannion, a league source told @spotrac.com . All three players will now be restricted free agents this offseason.

item.title: “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a …
item.description: To sweeten the deal, journalist Kevin O’Connor reported that Davis could also be lured to Dub Nation. However, NBA insider Marc Stein was quick to pump the brakes on those reports, explaining why such a blockbuster move is far from materializing. “Based on everything I was told tonight, I don’t think there is anything active there,” Marc Stein stated on The All-NBA Podcast. “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a lure for LeBron, hard to see that happening in that sequence… I think

item.title: Draymond Green's opt-out gives Warriors room to pursue Anthony Davis and recruit LeBron James
item.description: Shams Charania: The opt-out comes days after the Warriors said they expect Green to pick up the $27.7M option. Instead Green delivers Golden State time and space to pursue Davis with the Wizards and convince James to join Stephen Curry, Green and his old Lakers championship teammate in the Bay.

item.title: “This is potentially a very big domino in this NBA …
item.description: Brian Windhorst on what Draymond opting out means for LeBron: “This is potentially a very big domino in this NBA offseason… This opens the door for the Warriors to use their MLE with $15M… The Lakers want to meet with him and give him their plan but the way it was explained to me, LeBron has been unavailable for a meeting with the Lakers… I would just say this, stay tuned.”

item.title: They've got $48 million below the first apron to …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Golden State Warriors salary cap situation after Draymond Green opted out, clearing $27.7 million off their payroll. They've got $48 million below the first apron to budget re-signing him, Kristaps Porzingis, and LeBron James with the $15 million non-tax mid-level exception.

item.title: Draymond Green declines player option
item.description: Shams Charania: Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green is declining his $27.7 million player option to become a free agent, sources tell ESPN. This move gives the Warriors flexibility to pursue LeBron James in free agency and Anthony Davis via trade to form a Big 4.

item.title: The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: This is a huge development. The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for pursuing LeBron James AND Anthony Davis, which as I reported last night is their plan.

item.title: The move will likely open up the full $15M non tax …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Golden State was $20M below the first apron prior to Green declining his option. The move will likely open up the full $15M non tax midlevel exception to use and still have enough flexibility to build the bench.

item.title: LeBron James, Lakers have yet to discuss future, but that's expected to change following Warriors reports
item.description: Chris Haynes: LeBron James and Los Angeles Lakers still have yet to have a conversation regarding his future but that’s expected to change in light of Golden State Warriors’ strategic maneuvering to position themselves as a landing spot, sources tell me.

item.title: Rich Paul met with Wizards to discuss Anthony Davis' future with the franchise
item.description: Anthony Davis' agent, Rich Paul, met with the Washington Wizards on Sunday to discuss his future with the franchise. Davis is eligible this offseason for a four-year extension worth up to $275 million this offseason with the Wizards. As the Wizards recently signed Trae Young to a four-year, $212 million contract, Davis is clearly gauging whether he could receive a similar commitment from the franchise. Davis has also been the subject of trade rumors involving the Golden State Warriors with a pot

item.title: "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract …
item.description: "Anthony Davis just saw Trae Young get a $200 million new contract with the Wizards," said ESPN's Brian Windhorst on Monday's episode of Get Up. "Yesterday, Rich Paul, who represents Draymond Green and LeBron and Anthony Davis met with the Wizards to discuss the future. "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract that would make him very happy to be in Washington. Maybe the Wizards are prepared to do that. The Wizards want to win immediately. "If not, the Warriors could potentially co

item.title: 2026 First-round pick Bennett Stirtz: 'I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career'
item.description: During the pre-NBA Draft process, Bennett Stirtz worked out with the Chicago Bulls, Oklahoma City Thunder, Golden State Warriors, Memphis Grizzlies, Toronto Raptors and others. Now he’s in New York, getting ready for his name to be called at the 2026 NBA Draft. A few years ago, not even Stirtz thought this was possible. “Not at all,” Stirtz said. “I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career.”

item.title: Kings expected to be interested in Jonathan Kuminga
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Sacramento Kings had strong interest in Jonathan Kuminga before he was dealt from the Golden State Warriors to the Atlanta Hawks and are expected to be interested in him now as a free agent.

item.title: Porzingis is headed back to the Warriors on a two year …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Porzingis is headed back to the Warriors on a two year deal worth around $40 mil, according to league source. Second year is a player option. Warriors have thought all along that Porzingis fits what they want to do. Porzingis said repeatedly how much he loves Rick Celebrini.

item.title: Likely prohibits Golden State from using the full …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Warriors were eligible to reach an extension with Porzingis ahead of the veteran center hitting the open market on Tuesday night. The deal, according to @BobbyMarks42 , likely prohibits Golden State from using the full non-taxpayer mid-level exception.

item.title: They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Warriors cap situation after extending Kristaps Porzingis and De'Anthony Melton presumably opting out. The LeBron James-Anthony Davis pipedream is still alive. They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for Davis. Then they could fit James with $15 million non-taxpayer mid-level under the first apron hard cap IF Draymond Green takes the same contract as Porzingis, and IF they also find a taker for Moses Moody.

item.title: Kristaps Porzingis will return to Golden State
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Kristaps Porzingis has agreed to a two-year, $40 million contract extension to return to the Golden State Warriors through 2027-28, with a player option in the second season, agent Jeff Schwartz of Excel Sports Management tells ESPN.

item.title: Draymond Green expected to stay with Warriors
item.description: Green and the Warriors are expected to reunite, team sources said, with his exact contract -- length, first-year salary and full contract amount -- to be determined after the Warriors determine other business. They are still expected to pursue James, among other unrestricted free agents, once the market opens.

item.title: No tangible movement for Anthony Davis to Golden State talks
item.description: That could also mean exploring a trade for Washington center Anthony Davis to help lure James, though there has yet to be tangible movement on that front, league sources said.

item.title: Among notable names on #Warriors summer-league roster: …
item.description: Ron Kroichick: Among notable names on #Warriors summer-league roster: Will Richard & LJ Cryer from last season’s GSW team, Yaxel Lendeborg & Lajae Jones from last week’s draft, Graham Ike from Gonzaga and Chance McMillian from Vallejo/Texas Tech

item.title: Interesting twist to the Warriors' summer league …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: Interesting twist to the Warriors' summer league roster: Alex Toohey, their top second-round pick last year, is back after being injured and waived last season

item.title: De'Anthony Melton becomes unrestricted free agent
item.description: Keith Smith: De'Anthony Melton declined his player option with the Golden State Warriors, a league source told @spotrac . Melton will be an unrestricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Will Richard said one of the biggest things the …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Will Richard said one of the biggest things the coaching staff stressed to him this summer is to get stronger. He said he’s been spending a lot of time in the weight room doing squats and training.

item.title: A lot of guys have mentioned he’s even bigger than …
item.description: Nick Friedell: High praise for Yaxel after first day of practice. A lot of guys have mentioned he’s even bigger than they thought he was. And they can see that he has the ability to defend all over the floor. A lot of hype for his debut in Cali Classic/Vegas.

item.title: Dusty May could have been an option for Warriors?
item.description: ESPN’s Brian Windhorst revealed on the Hoop Collective podcast that Dusty May -- the newly hired Dallas Mavericks head coach and reigning national championship-winning coach at Michigan -- would have been on the Warriors' coaching short list had Kerr chosen not to return. “I think had Steve Kerr not re-signed in Golden State, Dusty May would have been on the Warriors’ short list,” Windhorst said. “I think he was on the short list a couple of other places, and he was clearly on the short list for

item.title: "It is possible that the Warriors could call the …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "It is possible that the Warriors could call the Wizards and make an offer and say we would love to trade for Anthony Davis, and that Anthony Davis could be traded and LeBron could come in free agency and those things could happen. Yes, it is possible... and then Draymond could then resign for a lower number and then add years onto the back to make himself whole. All of those things are possible. I will also tell you that the Warriors routinely, every transaction window, and wha

item.title: Unless LeBron wants to come on the minimum, no, you …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "I never rule anything out in this world, but you just look at the math of it. They are 27 million below the first apron. They are going to bring back Draymond Green. You don't leave 27 on the table to explore free agency. Even if he came back at 20 million, you are 7 million, and anything you use more than 6.1 of that non-tax mid-level, you get hard-capped. So when you look at it from that perspective, unless LeBron wants to come on the minimum, no, you don't have 15 million to off

item.title: The Wizards are over here saying like, what about us? …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "I think what Mike (Dunleavy) and the group don't want to do is trade for Anthony Davis and give up a ton and then don't have LeBron coming. So like, that would be probably the worst-case scenario to think, right? Because it's going to, because here's the thing, and I said this on TV today, it's like, everybody's making it, we're talking about this and it's great. And the Wizards are over here saying like, what about us? You know, like, what is the incentive for the Wizards to trade

item.title: League sources say that the Warriors certainly haven't …
item.description: League sources say that the Warriors certainly haven't been operating under the premise that they must adhere to any mandate that James and Davis are somehow a package deal. If James becomes truly available once free agency actually begins and receptive to such pitches, Golden State will be trying to convince him to sign for an estimated $15 million. And Green, to be clear, is going nowhere. He's expected to sign a new multi-year deal after the Warriors sort out the rest of their roster business

item.title: This is going end up with Draymond getting a new …
item.description: [Windhorst] "The people on the league I'm talking to today are giving me the side eye and they're going, this is going end up with Draymond getting a new contract in GS, LeBron getting a new contract in LA and AD getting a new contract in Washington."

item.title: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and asking how he would fit in playing with Steph, Draymond, LeBron and AD.

item.title: Warriors unlikely to move Jimmy Butler?
item.description: Behind the scenes, however, Golden State continues to signal a reluctance to use Butler's contract when he's injured for a move like that. Consider the strong relationships with Butler held by Warriors general manager Mike Dunleavy and coach Steve Kerr. Butler and Kerr are frequently in San Diego in the offseason and known to cross paths.

item.title: The Kings are not expected to pursue free agent …
item.description: On a related note, the Kings are not expected to pursue free agent forward Jonathan Kuminga, according to league sources who were granted anonymity to discuss free-agent scenarios before Tuesday. The Atlanta Hawks declined Kuminga’s team option Monday, making the 23-year-old an unrestricted free agent. The Kings attempted to acquire Kuminga last offseason via a sign-and-trade scenario but could not agree to a deal with Kuminga’s former team, the Golden State Warriors.

item.title: Anthony Davis to Warriors 'unlikely'
item.description: Brian Windhorst: The Warriors still have the ability to re-sign Draymond Green to a reasonable contract number and to try to add LeBron James, but it's really, really narrow and they would really need to make some other moves to build up their team. Right now, I'm taking the Anthony Davis thing off the front burner. I do not have data that says that that's likely to happen.

item.title: The actions were that Draymond Green opted out of …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: I like actions over words. The actions were that Draymond Green opted out of his contract. That gives the Warriors flexibility, but I don't think the Warriors think they're getting Anthony Davis. Do you want to know why I think they will? Because they signed Kristaps Porzingis. And this contract and Porzingis did not play a lot of games last year for anybody, much less for the Warriors. But you've got to have big men to compete in the Western Conference. They had to handle this

item.title: Draymond Green on opting out of his Warriors contract: It allows us to possibly do some things
item.description: Draymond Green: For me personally, you know, I’m always willing to work with the team on whatever is best, especially at this point in my career. So, my decision to opt out, was for a few reasons. As you all know, I have always taken the approach of working with the organization. I’ve been in one place for 14 years. It’s more of a family to me than anything. Like you’re talking people I’ve built relationships with over the course of the past 14 years. I view Golden State more like Michigan State

item.title: Warriors 'at the front of the line' in pursuit of LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Warriors are at the front of the line in pursuit of LeBron James in free agency and league sources tell @TheSteinLine that Golden State is not operating as though it has a mandate to also pursue an Anthony Davis trade.

item.title: LeBron James to Warriors not a done deal yet
item.description: Anthony Slater: Warriors decision makers have been given no indication at this hour that they are the firm LeBron James landing spot, per sources. There's a belief that they'll have plenty of appeal, but appears to be some level of recruitment required.

item.title: Golden State will certainly be pitching LeBron James …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Golden State will certainly be pitching LeBron James to join Stephen Curry and Draymond Green with the Warriors. From last night: "Golden State is known to be the league's most interested external suitor for the 41-year-old."

item.title: Cavaliers, Heat, Warriors expected to emerge as suitors for LeBron James
item.description: SportsCenter: I'm told this is a happiness-led decision for LeBron James. ... Wherever he lands in free agency, it will not be driven by money." "It's unclear what the timeline will be. Right now, he's in Ohio spending time with his family. He's gonna make a decision on his own time. But there's no question all of the NBA's contending teams are in the picture for LeBron James. Rich Paul will call LeBron with expressed interest as soon as free agency opens up tonight. Around the league, teams exp

item.title: The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James main priority is going to a team that has a chance at winning, per @ChrisBHaynes "I'm told he's going to be open minded and patient for his next team.... The one component a team is going to have to have to secure his service from what i'm told, is the team has to be competitive and have a realistic chance at winning.... The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can pretty much look and see of those 3 who has the best shot at winning... Money wasn't mentioned"

item.title: The Warriors are now signaling that Davis isn't in …
item.description: The Warriors are now signaling that Davis isn't in their plans, according to league sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations. Conversely, the Washington Wizards, the club that employs Davis, is very against the prospect of trading him to the Warriors (or, for now, anywhere), those sources said.

item.title: All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is …
item.description: Is LeBron’s interest contingent on the Warriors acquiring his old Lakers teammate and fellow Paul client Anthony Davis, which would take the Warriors putting Jimmy Butler into any offer with the Wizards for salary-matching purposes? And would the Warriors have to commit to a long-term extension with Davis as part of this mammoth undertaking? All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is mostly being assumed but has not been explicitly demanded by LeBron or Paul. And the Davis/Butler part of

item.title: But also, what better way for Paul to shake loose the …
item.description: Many league sources have long speculated that if and when LeBron and the Lakers soured on each other, the Warriors would be LeBron’s first choice, if he wanted to continue playing and certain conditions could be met. And I think Monday’s intrigue was an indication that he wants to continue playing, let’s put it that way. But also, what better way for Paul to shake loose the Lakers’ best offer than whispering that LeBron is extremely interested in wandering a bit north and joining up with Curry a

item.title: “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have interest in LeBron James, per @TheSteinLine “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but there is a line…. Cleveland is certainly going to try and convince him of a 3rd marriage, Miami has interest…. This might not necessarily happen tonight”

item.title: As I’ve said for months, I think Davis will be on the …
item.description: Are people in the Wizards organization laughing at these Anthony Davis to the Warriors rumors? — @Faazzla The Wizards have no interest in trading Anthony Davis. As I’ve said for months, I think Davis will be on the Wizards’ roster to begin the regular season.

item.title: NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 teams each got a $4.85 million payout. Cavaliers owe NBA-leading $68.671 million in tax payments. Warriors will pay $67.91 million as a repeater tax payer. Knicks owe $44.44 million after finishing $19.5 mil over tax line.

item.title: League sources told Sharania and ESPN's Anthony Slater …
item.description: League sources told Sharania and ESPN's Anthony Slater that a Warriors pursuit of Davis could be used as a motivational tactic to lure James to Golden State. The two won a championship together in 2020 with the Lakers and have remained close friends. However, there has yet to be tangible movement on the Davis front, league sources said.

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg said he didn’t hear from Green, after …
item.description: Draymond Green didn’t hold back when exposing his newest teammate on his podcast “The Draymond Green Show” on Tuesday. Yaxel Lendeborg said he didn’t hear from Green, after being selected as the No.11 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, Green publicly called him out on his podcast, “The Draymond Green Show,” and called him a “F— liar,” and went on to say, “It’s the Michigan way, of course it’s the Michigan way.”

item.title: That means that Porzingis is immediately …
item.description: Keith Smith: Note on Kristaps Porzingis' new contract with the Warriors: This is an extension (and it's already official) vs a new deal. The deal also didn't include greater than a 5% raise from Year 1 to Year 2. That means that Porzingis is immediately trade-eligible. No restrictions.

item.title: Kristaps Porzingis' deal with Warriors is an extension
item.description: Marc Stein: And: The more crucial factor, per a league source, is that Kristaps Porziņģis and the Warriors have done this deal as an extension rather than a Bird Rights contract.

item.title: Warriors, I'm told, were able to officially announce …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Warriors, I'm told, were able to officially announce Kristaps Porziņģis' new contract because -- while the start of NBA free agency is now 6 PM ET -- its annual moratorium on completing official roster business won't begin until after midnight. The moratorium runs July 1-6.

item.title: Shelburne: Warriors want commitment from LeBron James before pursuing Anthony Davis
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Ramona on Lebron possibly going to the Warriors: "From what I understand this is a sequencing issue. They would want a commitment from Lebron James before they would go make a move for somebody like Anthony Davis. As I understand it now there haven't been any substantive conversations between the Warriors and the Washington Wizards about Anthony Davis...yet"

item.title: Only $3 million is guaranteed for the second season
item.description: Anthony Slater: Pair of relevant notes on Kristaps Porzingis' new contract, per sources. 1. Only $3 million is guaranteed for the second season. 2. He's immediately trade eligible because of the structure of the two-year, $40 million extension (no more than 5% raise between yrs 1 and 2).

item.title: Steph Curry has been named a finalist for the Muhammad …
item.description: Dalton Johnson @DaltonJ_Johnson Steph Curry has been named a finalist for the Muhammad Ali Humanitarian Award, an incredibly special honor.

item.title: The Warriors are believed to have lined up an …
item.description: "It's going to happen on LeBron's timeline," said one team executive privy to the state of the sweepstakes. The Stein Line, though, has also learned this much: The Warriors are believed to have lined up an additional roster move or two if necessary to create additional financial flexibility in the event they do get word that LeBron James wants to make the Bay Area his (likely) final stop of a career that has outlasted every single star in this game before him.

item.title: Quinten Post is quietly accruing interest from a few …
item.description: Golden State reserve center Quinten Post is quietly accruing interest from a few teams out there after receiving a qualifying offer from the Warriors. He's not nearly as essential to competing in the top-heavy West as Kristaps Porziņģis. Post, after all, is currently only slated to be Golden State's third big man behind Porziņģis and Al Horford. Yet he does have some big fans within the organization thanks to his play as a spot-starter over his first two seasons as a Warrior.

item.title: Porzingis is guaranteed for only $3M in 2026-27 and …
item.description: Fred Katz: Funky contracts all around the league now. Adding to @anthonyVslater’s report on Kristaps Porzingis contract details: Porzingis is guaranteed for only $3M in 2026-27 and the Warriors have until July 7 to waive him. Extra flexibility for them.

item.title: In order for Kristaps Porzingis to have a partial …
item.description: Keith Smith: In order for Kristaps Porzingis to have a partial guarantee with an early trigger date for the 2027-28 season, the 2026-27 season has to be partially guaranteed with an early trigger date too. I get why it’s frustrating, but the Trade Machine is functioning as it’s supposed to.

item.title: Shams: LeBron James in no rush to make decision
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on LeBron James future: “One quote really sticks out to me. Rich Paul on June 2025 to me, ‘LeBron James wants to compete for a championship, he knows the Lakers are building for the future.’ When I talk to teams around the league it’s a few teams that really get narrowed. It’s the Golden State Warriors, Cleveland Cavaliers, and the Miami Heat as a perspective destination. I’m told LeBron it’s not in a rush.”

item.title: LeBron James and the Lakers ‘simply didn't see eye to …
item.description: NBA Base: LeBron James and the Lakers ‘simply didn't see eye to eye’ on what it meant to compete for a championship, per @ShamsCharania LeBron's next move will not be financially driven and is based entirely on his happiness and playing “meaningful basketball.” Last summer, Rich Paul basically challenged the Lakers' desire to win. Now, the Warriors, Cavs, and Heat are the top suitors.

item.title: Paul Pierce on LeBron James potentially joining Warriors: 'It starts to turn into a spectacle'
item.description: NBA Courtside: Paul Pierce says when he thinks about LeBron James joining the Warriors he cringes a little bit: “Because like it’s just like man, come on. How many like Hall of Famer superstars you want to team up with? It starts to turn into like a spectacle. You know, it starts to turn into that because you guys aren’t going to win. But you guys are two of the biggest superstars the NBA has ever had.” (Via @NFGShow)

item.title: Warriors always wanted De’Anthony Melton back at a …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Warriors always wanted De’Anthony Melton back at a cost-controlled price after he tested his market. Backcourt fit next to Steph Curry, beloved in locker room and he’s always spoken well of training staff.

item.title: De'Anthony Melton likely goes into the Warriors …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: De'Anthony Melton likely goes into the Warriors bi-annual exception. He can decline the player option next year and re-sign at fair value by accumulating Early Bird rights. They still preserve their mid-level exception for a potential LeBron James signing.

item.title: Warriors, De'Anthony Melton agree to two-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard De'Anthony Melton has agreed to a two-year, $11 million deal to return to the Golden State Warriors, sources tell ESPN. There is a player option in the season of the deal as well.

item.title: So now that he has done that, I will open the window …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "I have a confession. I have been thinking for nine months about LeBron playing for the Denver Nuggets. I refused to talk about it during the season, but I just thought as soon as they didn't extend LeBron last year and they extended Luka, I kind of knew it was probably going to come to an end. I thought about the Denver Nuggets because him alongside Jokic is an incredibly perfect fit. They are so intelligent, the way they see the game. And here is the thing, it was all predicat

item.title: Spurs not expected to go after LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Spurs’ deal with Tobias Harris slams the point home, but league sources say San Antonio is not expected to pursue LeBron James. @BennettDurando reported that Denver, by contrast, IS joining Golden State, Cleveland and Miami in this chase. More NBA: tinyurl.com/pebetpkn

item.title: Mavericks also acquire the draft rights to Tarik …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Mavericks also acquire the draft rights to Tarik Biberovic, sources said. The Warriors pick in 2030 is protected 1 to 20, then turns into a second if not conveyed.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks are trading AJ Johnson, a protected …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The Dallas Mavericks are trading AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 first-round pick (via Warriors) & two future second-round picks to the #Grizzlies in exchange for Santi Aldama & the draft rights to Tarik Biberovic, a person familiar with the deal confirms.

item.title: "It's about a 50/50 right now. It's between Cleveland …
item.description: 95.7 The Game: "It's about a 50/50 right now. It's between Cleveland and Golden State." Warriors insider @NickFriedell on what the chances are that Lebron James joins the Warriors (@WillardAndDibs)

item.title: While we're on it, I'm advocating LeBron go to Golden …
item.description: Lonzo Ball: While we're on it, I'm advocating LeBron go to Golden State."

item.title: "Certainly it would be fun to see he and Steph playing …
item.description: Rick Barry: "Certainly it would be fun to see he and Steph playing together. They may set a record for the oldest team age-wise in the history of the NBA. It's kind of crazy with all the old guys that they're signing. But the guy is remarkable. To have the season he had, the numbers he put up last year at 41 years of age, very few players in the history of the league ever put up numbers like that."

item.title: Mavericks targeting Anfernee Simons
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks have emerged as a suitor for Anfernee Simons, league sources say. As @JakeLFischer reported yesterday, Golden State and Miami have continued to pursue the free agent scoring guard who finished last season with Chicago.

item.title: Trey Murphy drawing trade interest from Celtics, Pistons, Warriors, Clippers, Hawks
item.description: Teams that are in the mix for Murphy to varying degrees now are the Celtics, Pistons, Warriors, Clippers, and Hawks, league sources told HoopsHype. The Celtics have monitored Murphy’s trade market, and following the Brown trade for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks, they have gained extra draft capital that New Orleans has desired in any trade talks. The Pistons have consistently circled the wagons on Murphy in trade talks, but felt the price was too high. With the a

item.title: Warriors, Heat and Nuggets among teams interested in Anfernee Simons before signing with Philadelphia
item.description: Michael Scotto: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.

item.title: Pat Spencer joins Phoenix
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Pat Spencer has agreed to a two-way NBA contract with the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. Spencer's role grew across three seasons in Golden State, where he averaged 7.2 points and 18.6 minutes last season.

item.title: Daeqwon Plowden signs with Sacramento
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Sacramento Kings are signing Daeqwon Plowden to a 2-year, $5.1 million deal. After two-way deals with the Warriors, Hawks, and Kings, Plowden gets a standard NBA deal after averaging 10.8 points last season. Agent Drew Kelso of @OneMotiveSports will finalize the deal

item.title: The asking price for Trey Murphy was three first-round …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: The asking price for Trey Murphy was three first-round picks at the trade deadline. The hope would be two But you do what it takes to get a talent like Trey. He's the perfect bridge the Warriors need, and have needed. Do what it takes, as every team should be doing with him

item.title: Warriors believe they're not LeBron James' top choice?
item.description: The Warriors, meanwhile, have been given indications from James that they remain on his short list, but team sources have expressed a recent belief that they aren't necessarily at the top of that short list, as James continues to survey the landscape.

item.title: “Rich Paul got a dozen of calls, pitches, offers …
item.description: NBA Base: Shams: “Rich Paul got a dozen of calls, pitches, offers already from contending teams, teams with aspirations… right now, the three that I mentioned — Golden State, Cleveland, Miami — those are seen as some of the types of teams that make logical sense for LeBron James.”

item.title: It must be said that there is most certainly a …
item.description: It had been anticipated for literally weeks that ever-aggressive Golden State would be at the front of the line to try to sign James if he actually created that opportunity by parting ways with the Lakers. Yet it must be said that there is most certainly a tangible line of teams forming. Ditto for a growing sense that LeBron's hometown Cleveland Cavaliers — despite financial limitations to rival Golden State's — might just have the capability to ultimately barge their way past everyone even thou

item.title: Carmelo Anthony: 'I would love to see him and Steph just go. They've been wanting to do it for a while'
item.description: Carmelo Anthony: "I would love to see him and Steph just go. They've been wanting to do it for a while. They've been on the Olympic team. Just go out there, you guys ride this shit out, finish it together. I don't know if Steph, this is it for Steph. I'm just saying like you guys are now at that point where you can actually go and try to develop something throughout the course of the year to win a championship."

item.title: Wolves, Warriors interested in Rui Hachimura?
item.description: As the market has developed, sources say Hachimura's representation has now been in contact with Minnesota and Golden State as well. The Timberwolves have a well-chronicled need for a power forward after committing to trade away Randle and Naz Reid so they can pair LaMelo Ball with Anthony Edwards and Hachimura is said to stand as their current top choice at the position.

item.title: Draymond Green on Jaylen Brown for Paul George trade: No disrespect to Paul George, but this isn’t an even swap
item.description: Draymond Green on Jaylen Brown for Paul George trade: No disrespect to Paul George, but this isn’t an even swap. I got a lot of love for PG. but like myself, PG is 36 years old. Not like myself. I’m going into year 15. I think PG is going into year 17. And Jaylen Brown, not even 30 years old. He’s 29 years old and he’s going into year 11. So, and by the way, he won finals MVP two years ago. I think he just made All-NBA second team this year. Was arguably controversially possibly could have been

item.title: I still think people don’t understand the force, the …
item.description: Draymond Green on impact LeBron James carries: I still think people don’t understand the force, the weight that this guy carry, you know, from a overall standpoint, you know, the economics that he bring to a team, the economic impact that he may have on the city. And then also the basketball like anytime LeBron James is on the floor, A, your team have a chance to win, but B, you have to account for him in a major way. And I think personally the Lakers are going to feel that next year.

item.title: I give him two to three more years, and I want to save …
item.description: If I were to punch you on the spot with LeBron, who do you think he’s gonna end up signing with, and how many more years do you think he plays? Mario Chalmers: I give him two to three more years, and I want to save Miami, but I have a gut feeling that it might be Golden State.

item.title: Ernesto “Ernny” Plascencia, a close friend of Jimmy …
item.description: Ernesto “Ernny” Plascencia, a close friend of Jimmy Butler who has regularly attended Warriors games since Butler’s acquisition in February 2025, was among six indicted Monday on federal charges related to an alleged sports gambling scheme.

item.title: The indictment does not name Butler, and the six-time …
item.description: Plascencia has never been an employee of Butler’s or any of Butler’s businesses, according to a person with knowledge of their association. Nor is he a Warriors employee. The indictment does not name Butler, and the six-time All-Star hasn’t been accused of any wrongdoing.

item.title: Shams: LeBron James deciding between Cavs, Heat, Nuggets, Sixers, Wolves, Warriors
item.description: Fullcourtpass: LeBron James will be making his free agent decision between the following six NBA teams, per @ShamsCharania - Cavaliers - Heat - Nuggets - Sixers - Timberwolves - Warriors

item.title: “I would say probably like a B,” Lendeborg said when …
item.description: Lendeborg played 22 minutes and scored a game-high 19 points on a perfect 6-of-6 shooting while going 4 of 4 from deep. Along with his scoring, Lendeborg led the Warriors in rebounds (five) and assists (six), plus he blocked a shot and had one steal. “I would say probably like a B,” Lendeborg said when assessing his debut. “I messed up a lot of defensive rotations. That’s kind of taken a while for me to get the rotations down. I played a fairly well game until the fourth quarter. Kind of went a

item.title: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources …
item.description: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources say, all still believe that they are in this race to some degree and getting legitimate consideration from James and his camp alongside the Cavaliers, Warriors and Heat.

item.title: Teams believe Cavs are favorites for LeBron James?
item.description: Yet there is certainly a growing belief among teams competing for James' signature that the lure of the 41-year-old playing out his final chapter with the team that drafted him — compared to, say, early favorites Golden State — is looking more and more like the scenario to beat.

item.title: "People don't understand the weight that LeBron James …
item.description: While James' move surely will have major implications for the league in several ways, current Warriors free agent Draymond Green recently said on the "Draymond Green Show" that people don't understand just how much things change with James moving around. "People don't understand the weight that LeBron James carries," Green said. "Still, after 24 years, I still think people don't understand the force, the weight that this guy carries. From an overall standpoint, the economics that he brings to a

item.title: Many teams still believe they have a chance at LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Sixers, the Timberwolves, the Heat, your Nuggets, based on everything I have been told all weekend, they all still believe they are in with the chance. I think the Warriors were portrayed as an early favorite, not the early favorite, an early favorite, because they were the team that we knew was determined to go after LeBron if he parted with the Lakers. We had known that for weeks leading up to LeBron and Rich Paul actually informing the Lakers, yes, we are definitely moving on

item.title: I just think that there are viable cases to be made …
item.description: Joe Vardon: "I told Rich that I thought this was the best or the most unique and that that had never been done before, what he did. It was absolutely incredible and unique, that entire message and the entire podcast, just to be so open about it. I think a lot of teams really are in the mix. I just think that there are viable cases to be made for Cleveland, Philadelphia, Miami, Golden State, and Dallas and the Timberwolves. I think those all those teams really are in play and I just feel like Ric

item.title: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is that that 2017-18 …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "The reality is that that 2017-18 Warriors team is a cheat code. I mean, they literally made the NBA say, you know what, forget vets, we're going to go all young guys on the bench. I mean, think about when that team came together. Only four teams thought they had a chance to really make a run: Warriors, Rockets, Cavs, and a coin flip of the East team was probably the Celtics because they were just prideful. Everyone else, they just started packing it in and saying like, we're j

item.title: Tristan Thompson: "If they win that (in 2016), …
item.description: Tristan Thompson: "If they win that (in 2016), Draymond Green get MVP. He had 30, 10, and 10 or something like that."

item.title: Quinten Post signing three-year, $30 million offer sheet with Grizzlies
item.description: Shams Charania: Restricted free agent Quinten Post is signing a three-year, $30 million contract offer sheet with the Memphis Grizzlies, sources tell ESPN. The Golden State Warriors have until 11:59pm ET on Tuesday to match.

item.title: George Roussakis and Mark Bartelstein of …
item.description: Shams Charania: George Roussakis and Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports landed the offer sheet for Post on Monday morning. The 7-footer averaged 7.8 points, 3.8 rebounds and 36.4% 3-point shooting across his first two NBA seasons with the Warriors, who selected him No. 52 overall in 2024.

item.title: This contract for Quentin Post will go into the …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: This contract for Quentin Post will go into the Grizzlies mid-level exception. If matched, it’ll be replenished for different moves. If unmatched, they’ll need to acquire Isaiah Stewart with the big $29 million trade exception or by expanding the Santi Aldama trade.

item.title: I suspect the Warriors won’t match as they need to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: I suspect the Warriors won’t match as they need to maintain cap flexibility for other potential moves.

item.title: The Warriors have been bracing for a Quinten Post …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Warriors have been bracing for a Quinten Post offer sheet for several days, source say, while Golden State remains entrenched in the LeBron James free agency sweepstakes.

item.title: Last time a player signed an offer sheet of $5M+ per …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Some context on the Quinten Post offer sheet in Memphis. * Last time a player signed an offer sheet of $5M+ per season was Matisse Thybulle with Dallas in 2023. * Last time an offer sheet went unmatched was 2020 (Bogdan Bogdanovic with Sacramento/Atlanta)

item.title: @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying …
item.description: Marc Stein: League sources tell @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying in Los Angeles and chose the Clippers over interest from Brooklyn and Dallas as well as interest we previously reported from Golden State/Minnesota.

item.title: Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized …
item.description: Sam Amick: Story on Rui Hachimura signing with the Clippers, at @TheAthletic , with @DanWoikeSports and @LawMurrayTheNU Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized Clippers over interest from Brooklyn, San Antonio, Golden State, Minnesota, and Portland.

item.title: Only the first season of Quinten Post’s three-year, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Only the first season of Quinten Post’s three-year, $30 million offer sheet from Memphis is guaranteed, per sources. Yet there are ~$1.3M in unlikely incentives each season if Post were to make an All-Defense Team, so that extra money would count against the Warriors’ apron

item.title: Warriors expected to check in on DeMar DeRozan
item.description: Anthony Slater: You know I see the Miami Heat. I anticipate them being interested potentially in DeMar DeRozan. The Warriors have already been told it's a guy they will look into at some point in the coming days, especially if they do not get LeBron James. There's some heartwarming potential landing spots right. You know the Clippers play in his hometown the Raptors. He's so famous. Those are two teams that compete that could use him. And the reality is he should be coming at such a bargain that

item.title: Donovan Mitchell would embrace Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James
item.description: Donovan Mitchell also would embrace the Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James if James makes the decision to sign with Cleveland, according to sources. The Cavaliers are among several leading suitors for James, joining Miami, Golden State, Philadelphia and Minnesota.

item.title: Kristaps Porzingis' contract with the Golden State …
item.description: Keith Smith: Kristaps Porzingis' contract with the Golden State Warriors becomes fully guaranteed for $20 million today. This is a quirk of Porzingis' extension. In order for the 2027-28 season to be partially guaranteed with a July 7, 2027 guarantee date, this season had to mirror it.

item.title: “The Heat, Warriors, Cavaliers, 76ers, Timberwolves, …
item.description: Heat Central: Marc Stein on the interested LeBron teams and their free agent spending: “The Heat, Warriors, Cavaliers, 76ers, Timberwolves, and Nuggets, all of these teams are kind of watching every dollar right now to try and maintain absolute maximum flexibility in the event that LeBron says ‘yes, I’d like to come there.’”

item.title: The field is set for the celebrity golf tournament at …
item.description: The field is set for the celebrity golf tournament at Edgewood Tahoe with fan favorites Charles Barkley, 2023 champion Steph Curry and three-time champion Tony Romo among the 90 celebrities competing. The American Century Championship is set for Wednesday-Sunday at Edgewood Tahoe with 90 entrants as of Monday, July 6.

item.title: Lakers acquire one of the top available backup …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers acquire one of the top available backup centers, and Looney, a three-time NBA champion, provides experience and pick-and-roll play for Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves after all of those years running screens for Stephen Curry, Klay Thompson, Kevin Durant, Draymond Green and Andre Iguodala in the Bay Area.

item.title: LeBron James to spend time with Draymond Green this week
item.description: Heat Central: Draymond Green and LeBron James are hanging out this week, per @ShamsCharania 🚨

item.title: Warriors believe they aren’t at the top of LeBron’s …
item.description: NBA Courtside: The Warriors believe they aren’t at the top of LeBron’s list unless they can package Anthony Davis, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: DeMar DeRozan interested in going back to Raptors?
item.description: While DeRozan is interested in reuniting with the Raptors, NBA insider Jake Fischer revealed that the former NBA star might likely join teams that will fail to land James in this year's free agency. “He’s almost certainly going to be on a vet minimum somewhere else. Could it be Toronto? ... DeMar has interest in going back to the Raptors, but I think DeMar’s most likely landing spots are going to be teams that don’t get LeBron James," Fischer revealed on Bleacher Report. “I think the Miamis, Cle

item.title: Warriors believe they are not on LeBron James' list unless they can trade for Anthony Davis
item.description: "I think LeBron's been open-minded, but the Warriors' whole plan going into free agency was, 'Okay, let's see if we can get Stephen Curry, Draymond Green, LeBron James, and Anthony Davis,'" said ESPN's Shams Charania on Stephen A. Smith's radio show. "I think the big draw for LeBron with the Warriors would be seeing if there's an avenue to be a package deal with Davis," added Charania. "Short of that, I don't think the Warriors are high on his list, and I think the Warriors themselves believe th

item.title: The Golden State Warriors decided not to match Quinten …
item.description: ‪Shams Charania: The Golden State Warriors decided not to match Quinten Post's three-year, $30 million offer sheet with the Grizzlies, allowing the 7-footer to join Memphis, sources tell ESPN. The Grizzlies add Post to a reshaped frontline that includes No. 3 pick Cameron Boozer, Zach Edey, Isaiah Stewart and

item.title: First time since 2020 that an offer sheet of $5M plus …
item.description: Bobby Marks: First time since 2020 that an offer sheet of $5M plus goes unmatched. However, the Bogdan Bogdanovic (Sac to Atl) unmatched sheet was during the 2017 CBA and different set of rules.

item.title: Despite some league sources believing that an …
item.description: Part of LeBron’s appeal to the Warriors is the low risk. It would be a short-term signing without the loss of a draft pick to acquire him. Despite some league sources believing that an asset-dependent trade for Anthony Davis might be necessary to lure James, the Warriors haven’t been told that directly and haven’t been engaging Washington on it, team sources said.

item.title: Since LeBron’s 2020 Lakers title, the last six NBA …
item.description: Since LeBron’s 2020 Lakers title, the last six NBA champions have won without luring accomplished free agents. Instead, the teams have largely drafted stars (Bucks, Warriors, Nuggets, Celtics) or assembled lineups by picking up players other teams were willing to part with (Knicks). The Thunder have built a perennial powerhouse by doing a bit of both, while the Spurs are tied with OKC as 2027/28 favorites thanks to a run of draft success.

item.title: Stephen Curry on pairing with LeBron James: 'We obviously would love to play together. Hopefully it happens'
item.description: Steph Curry on the possibility of playing with LeBron James: “I’d say more so I’m interested to just play golf with LeBron. We’ll handle the basketball stuff, but I want to see the golf LeBron free agent. He’s out here really grinding on the game. But I’m sure we obviously would love to play together. I mean, hopefully it happens. But he’s deserved the opportunity and the right to take his time with the decision.”

item.title: The Timberwolves are very much in the mix for LeBron …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "We are recording this on Tuesday evening, and so there is certainly any possibility that something happens tonight, tomorrow, whenever, to change things. But I would say that as things stand right now, one, it seems to be the perception around the league that LeBron is going to take his time with this decision. He is going to go through all of the options, talk things over with Rich Paul and his camp, and just really try to get a handle on what the best situation is for him. An

item.title: It’s like Minnesota kicking themselves over the KAT …
item.description: Kevin Garnett on Jaylen Brown trade: It’s like Minnesota kicking themselves over the KAT trade. I think Boston gonna look back on this. It’s like when KD left Golden State and then he turned around like what the fu*k, like this one of the moments where you don’t break a great thing up. This is a great thing. They just had to you know and then two, man. Keep it real man those two have been tested through the think time and time. So this is just relationship it ain’t always going to be great. You

item.title: Warriors bring back Charles Bassey
item.description: Shams Charania: The Golden State Warriors and center Charles Bassey have agreed on a one-year deal, bringing the big man back to the Bay Area for his sixth NBA season, sources tell ESPN. Bassey returns to Golden State in wake of Quinten Post joining the Grizzlies on an offer sheet.

item.title: “When I was there,” Lendeborg said, shaking his head …
item.description: His grind comes from his mother. He watched it live. The 12-hour shifts at VoiceComm began near dawn. Warehouse work reaches the bone, leaving feet aching, backs stiffened and spirits weary. At 4 p.m., she commuted home with people who left work early to beat traffic, and she’d just worked overtime moving electronics through a cavernous depot. “When I was there,” Lendeborg said, shaking his head as he remembered, “it was really 12-hour shifts. I’m like, ‘Man. This is hard labor.’ And she’s been

item.title: “If we’re talking strictly basketball, you don’t want …
item.description: Heat Central: Rich Paul on LeBron to the Warriors: “If we’re talking strictly basketball, you don’t want to play them. You definitely don’t want to play them in a playoff series. You don’t want to get to the trade deadline and have little surface edges type of moves made. You talk about just basketball brilliance of mind and experience, production. It’s pretty tough, Max.”

item.title: Bill Simmons on LeBron's free agency: 'The Cleveland thing is done'
item.description: Bill Simmons claimed that James going home to reunite with the Cleveland Cavaliers for the second time has already been determined. "The thing that happened to poor Golden State is, I think they thought they were getting LeBron and potentially [Anthony Davis] and now, it's pretty clear they were being used by leverage, as leverage, as LeBron goes back to Cleveland," the NBA pundit first said on the latest episode of his The Bill Simmons Podcast. When pressed by Joe House about the leverage he wa

item.title: LeBron James golfing with Draymond Green in Puerto …
item.description: LeBron James golfing with Draymond Green in Puerto Rico amid Warriors rumors

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed forward Tarik Biberovic (TAH-rick bee-BEHR-oh-vitch). Per team policy, terms of the deal were not disclosed. Dallas acquired the rights to Biberović along with forward Santi Aldama from Memphis as part of a six-team trade that was announced on Wednesday. The Mavericks sent guard AJ Johnson, a top-20 protected 2030 first-round pick (via Golden State) and a 2029 second-round pick (via Houston) to the Grizzlies as part of the deal.

item.title: "As I've been saying since 2013, Steph Curry, no one …
item.description: A decade after Kevin Durant signed with the Golden State Warriors in an all-time "if you can't beat them, join them" move, LeBron James is contemplating a similar relocation. Andre Iguodala, the 2015 Finals MVP in the first of the Warriors' three titles over James' Cleveland Cavaliers, says that James' interest in Golden State is a testament to Stephen Curry. "As I've been saying since 2013, Steph Curry, no one understands how great he is," Iguodala told ESPN on Friday after the National Basketb

item.title: "He's like, 'Steph Curry, if he's going to get another …
item.description: Iguodala, who is being succeeded as NBPA executive director by former Warriors chief legal officer David Kelly, said that a recent text exchange with a friend crystalized the magnitude of James' interest in the Warriors. "He's like, 'Steph Curry, if he's going to get another one of the greatest players of their generation to play on his team, what does it say about him?'" Iguodala said.

item.title: "And there's some things about him that you can't get. …
item.description: "We have to look back like, 'Whoa,'" Iguodala said of his former teammate. "We'll still see his effect [on the game] 30, 40 years from now. People who never thought of playing in the NBA are trying to get there now because of him. There's only one LeBron James. A lot of people can have Steph's stature. So just like you when you have Yao Ming, you got 2 billion more people interested in basketball, you got a bunch of Stephs walking around. "And there's some things about him that you can't get. He

item.title: "He's very much so deserving of it, and it's the …
item.description: Years after competing fiercely with James on the Finals stage, Iguodala also offered praise for how James is handling his free agency, heading into what will be a record-breaking 24th season. "He's very much so deserving of it, and it's the opportunity or even the thought of it. You can just feel the excitement from the fans," Iguodala said of James' free agency frenzy. "One thing I've learned in this tenure and working with the league and [commissioner] Adam [Silver], it's we got to do a better

item.title: Moses Moody addresses trade rumors
item.description: But the Warriors love Moses Moody. He’s been a model role player during his five seasons in the Bay. He’s a 24-year-old, 6-foot-5 wing who shot 40 percent from 3 last season and set career-highs in scoring average (12.1) and minutes per game (25.7). “I’m curious, so I had some conversations just trying to figure out what’s going on,” Moody said. “But I don’t call Rich every day. I’m not in it like that because I ain’t got nothing to do with how it’s gonna go down. So I’m more so just watching. …

item.title: Moody said he hasn’t heard from the Warriors. He said …
item.description: He’d much rather talk about the partnership with Trades 4 Life that will teach people trade and life skills during the youth summit than spend too much time worrying about if he’ll be traded. Other players find trade speculation stressful, a reasonable response to the sudden life upheaval that ensues. But Moody’s having too much fun creating something for young people in Little Rock. Moody said he hasn’t heard from the Warriors. He said he checked in with Rich Paul, his agent, but has largely av

item.title: Brian Windhorst poured cold water Friday on …
item.description: ESPN’s Brian Windhorst poured cold water Friday on speculation that James is attempting to recruit longtime Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green away from the franchise, saying he has not heard anything to substantiate the claim. “I have not heard that from a credible source,” Windhorst said Friday on ESPN Cleveland. “If you’re asking me whether that was something that could happen, that’s a different conversation. But I have not heard that from a credible source.”

item.title: “Conversations that I don’t need to be a part of,” …
item.description: The four-time NBA champion addressed the long-running conversation around his lack of preferential foul treatment during an interview with NBC Sports Bay Area’s Monte Poole -- and his response was as unbothered as his on-court demeanor suggests. “Conversations that I don’t need to be a part of,” Curry told Poole on "Dubs Talk" at the American Century Championship in South Lake Tahoe. “I play basketball; figure it out as I go. I try my best not to complain out there on the court and just figure o

item.title: David Kelly criticized the NBA's salary cap and luxury …
item.description: National Basketball Players Association executive director David Kelly criticized the NBA's salary cap and luxury tax system on Friday, arguing that the newly implemented "second apron" must be "softened" or removed. "We are not fans of the second apron," Kelly said. "We did not propose the second apron. We should have done a better job of fighting back against the second apron. In the future, we will have a much more unified union, and we will do a better job of fighting back. ... We're seeing

item.title: “He is Heat royalty for Micky Arison, Nick Arison, Pat …
item.description: Heat Central: Marc Stein on the reports that former Heat players like Tyler Herro and Jimmy Butler weren’t the biggest fans of Bam Adebayo being chosen as the ‘guy’ for the Miami Heat: “He is Heat royalty for Micky Arison, Nick Arison, Pat Riley. He is the modern day Udonis Haslem. They consider him the absolute franchise pillar. I’ve always heard nothing but he’s held in high regards by his teammates… He is the untouchable there. That part is for sure. He is an extension of Pat Riley, Spo, Owne

item.title: LeBron James to the Warriors still a possibility?
item.description: NBA Courtside: Jake Fischer on LeBron and the Warriors: “I do think the Warriors are still as involved as any team. I want to say that too. As much as the reunion with Cleveland and Miami would be storybook, this new all star team, traveling team USA kind of circus, high entertainment value thing that would exist is GS, is something that is still very much on the table.”

item.title: Draymond Green says the problem he has with Stephen A …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green says the problem he has with Stephen A is he disrespects NBA players as if he is a player himself: “You very much so right up there at the top… A lot of players having a relationship with you, were facts. Until you started to disrespect players as if you were a player. There’s a fine line… Stephen A the problem I have is you get upset about the very thing that you do. You get so upset and bent out of shape about the very same thing that you do.” (Via @DraymondShow)

item.title: Frank Vogel to join Warriors as lead assistant coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Golden State Warriors are hiring Frank Vogel as their lead assistant coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Vogel spent last season as an assistant coach with the Dallas Mavericks. Vogel won the 2020 NBA championship with the Los Angeles Lakers as their head coach.

item.title: Draymond Green, Udonis Haslem reignite beef over Jordan Poole incident
item.description: “I remember when I got into it with Poole… I remember one person when the Jordan Poole incident happened with me who was really, really outspoken about it, and it really bothered me, was Udonis Haslem,” Draymond Green said. “I saw Udonis Haslem get into it with guys on teams that was younger than him before. Ala Jimmy Butler. Then he came out like, ‘That’s ridiculous! You get into it with your young guy I would never. I can’t believe you did that…' So when I first saw this, I was like damn. This

item.title: Udonis Haslem: @Money23Green I see some things just …
item.description: Udonis Haslem: @Money23Green I see some things just don’t change. lol. You was on sucka shit four years ago when you swung on Jordan Poole and you on sucka shit now. I usually don’t engage but since you went so far left to get my attention here it is!!! If you think your big 32 year old, 3 or 4 rings at the time having ass swinging on a 23 year old Jordan Poole at the time is the same then you are even more delusional that I thought.

item.title: The shit you pull in golden state and that shit he …
item.description: Udonis Haslem: First of all Bam is 28. Tyler is 26. Neither one of them got it all figured out yet. Maybe I was giving you too much credit at the time cause I thought you did so that’s why I commented. 2nd, anyone who knows bam knows he’s strong as baby bear. If he would’ve unloaded on Tyler 100% it’s over. You fired off on that young boy like it was a club punch and you never met him before in your life. 3rd yeah I did go at Jimmy. You damn right. The shit you pull in golden state and that shit

item.title: You brought me into this and I am retired and out the …
item.description: Udonis Haslem : Ion really vibe you and I think you know that so unless it’s me on prime talking hoops I won’t mention you at all. You brought me into this and I am retired and out the way. I suggest you keep it pushing cause I ain’t giving out no more hall passes bra!

item.title: 'Draymond Green is doing everything he can to recruit …
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: 'Draymond Green is doing everything he can to recruit LeBron James to the Warriors' @shamscharania talks to @termineradio, @jumpshot8, and Ryan McDonough about the latest on the possibility of LeBron signing with Golden State. 'Draymond to my knowledge, he is fully committed to going back to Golden State. He said on the record multiple times he wants to retire a Warrior.'

item.title: “Guys like Steph and Luka see a lot of different …
item.description: Going back to hoops and what you said about Luka and the gravity he attracts. You also played with Steph and saw how his gravity works. What notes do you take from that experience, and how can you apply it with how you play with Luka? Kevon Looney: “Guys like Steph and Luka see a lot of different coverages. As a big man in the pick-and-roll, you have to be ready for all of those different reads. Is he getting blitzed? Are they playing into drop? You’re going to see all sorts of different coverag

item.title: Shams: LeBron James wants to go somewhere he can compete for a championship and find happiness
item.description: Heat Culture: Shams on LeBron James: “It’s decision time for LeBron James… The Leading suitors have been CLE, MIA, GSW, PHI, and MIN…. LeBron James has made it clear privately that he wants to go somewhere he can compete for a championship. Somewhere he feels he finds that happiness from a team environment and CULTURE that he can uplift”

item.title: The Golden State Warriors signed Charles Bassey to a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Golden State Warriors signed Charles Bassey to a one-year, $2.85 million deal, which is partially guaranteed for $1.4 million, league sources told @hoopshype . Bassey helps fill the void of Quinten Post, who recently signed with the Grizzlies. ESPN first reported an agreement.

item.title: Sierra: Inspired by 82-0, we have put together this …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Inspired by 82-0, we have put together this game we have called 73-9. You have $100 million to spend on players to try to beat the 2015-16 Warriors. It's a long shot, especially because you are constrained by money. Shoot your shot, though! Link: hoopsmatic.com/73-9-game

item.title: Inspired by 82-0, we have put together this game we …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Inspired by 82-0, we have put together this game we have called 73-9. You have $100 million to spend on players to try to beat the 2015-16 Warriors. It's a long shot, especially because you are constrained by money. Shoot your shot, though!

item.title: Western conference executives believe LeBron James will sign with Warriors
item.description: Some executives are skeptical of the growing narrative that a third Cleveland stint is a certainty. Multiple Western Conference executives told Front Office Sports they expect James to sign with the Warriors over the Cavaliers for his 24th NBA season. While Cleveland has been the favorite on prediction markets—and Bill Simmons said the move was a “done deal”—some executives believe Golden State is still a real player for James’s services.

item.title: The Warriors, most of all owner Joe Lacob, appear …
item.description: What can be passed on here as confirmed reporting is that Golden State — as much as it has been operating with tangible concern that Cleveland is the closest thing to a frontrunner — has not relented in its pursuit. The Warriors, most of all owner Joe Lacob, appear determined to stay in it until the end.

item.title: “I would be stunned if he picked a team that wasn’t …
item.description: While another league source expects James to sign with the Cavaliers, the consensus among executives polled by FOS is that James will likely pick between the Warriors and Cavaliers. “I would be stunned if he picked a team that wasn’t Golden State or Cleveland,” a Western Conference executive said.

item.title: A third (and bow-tying) marriage with the Cleveland …
item.description: The sentiment I continue to hear from various teams chasing James has not really changed from the way Jake Fischer and I co-reported it below on July 4: A third (and bow-tying) marriage with the Cleveland Cavaliers continues to be regarded as “the scenario to beat” by the the teams pursuing James that have been most frequently mentioned: Golden State, Miami, Philadelphia, Minnesota and Denver. To beat. Not necessarily unbeatable.

item.title: “We’re getting close to finalizing the roster here.”
item.description: Fullcourtpass: “We’re getting close to finalizing the roster here.” - Mike Dunleavy Jr on the Warriors 👀 (h/t @MindOfBron)

item.title: James doesn’t appear to be in any rush. This was shown …
item.description: The entire basketball world is waiting on LeBron James to decide which team he’ll sign with in free agency before what will be his 24th NBA season. However, James doesn’t appear to be in any rush. This was shown by a golf outing that he had on July 14, alongside Golden State Warriors star forward Draymond Green and world-class comedian Kevin Hart, both of whom are close friends with James.

item.title: I don't really have any like MVP kind of things. I …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Yaxel Lendeborg on his rookie year goals: "Try to slow the game down. I don't really have any like MVP kind of things. I just want to be a role model to the kids, I wanna be somebody that the kids look up to and wanna be like...try to be as simple as possible"

item.title: I’ve had Miami tell me it’s Cleveland. I’ve had …
item.description: Sam Amick on LeBron James: I’ve had Miami tell me it’s Cleveland. I’ve had Cleveland tell me it’s Miami. And the Warriors have seemingly had a lot of pessimism recently.

item.title: Stephen Curry took home another award Tuesday night, …
item.description: Golden State Warriors star and former two-time MVP Stephen Curry took home another award Tuesday night, receiving the Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian Award. Curry was recognized for his impact, creating the "Eat Learn Play" foundation. The former Davidson product beat out two NFL players for the award, as Damar Hamlin (Buffalo Bills) and the recently retired Adam Thielen were the two other finalists.

item.title: Warriors feel like LeBron James is probably joining an Eastern Conference team?
item.description: Heat Central: “I’m hearing today that the Warriors internally feel like they’re a dark horse at this point. That LeBron James is probably going out East.” — @MarcJSpears on LeBron to Golden State rumors

item.title: "Don't ever forget that you have a platform, you have …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Steph Curry: "Don't ever forget that you have a platform, you have a voice, you have everything that you need to make a true impact and I encourage you to find your Oakland"

item.title: Stephen Curry became Stephen Curry. To be apart of …
item.description: Heat Central: Bam Adebayo speaks on watching Steph Curry light up Serbia in the Olympics: “To be apart of something special like that, obviously when everybody gets involved and it’s team USA, it’s about winning. The standard is Gold. To be apart of that run, we had the doubt in our mind down 15 going into the 4th. Stephen Curry became Stephen Curry. To be apart of those type of moments and watch somebody be at that highest stage, excel like that was pretty exciting.”

item.title: A reunion between James and his Team USA coach and …
item.description: Golden State Warriors head coach Steve Kerr seems, at least in jest, to have the answer to the question currently stalling the last moves of NBA free agency. Where will LeBron James sign to play next season? In a video posted to X, fans caught Kerr in his car and asked him to sign James. A reunion between James and his Team USA coach and teammate — and longtime rivals — in Kerr and Stephen Curry at Golden State has long been rumored. “Oh, we got him,” Kerr said.

item.title: “It’s going to be pretty fun wherever I land… I seen …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James: “It’s going to be pretty fun wherever I land… I seen something on social media that wherever I go we’d be in the play in or some sh*t like that and I’m not a factor anymore. That’s just a little motivation… I’ve heard Warriors, I’ve heard Philly, Miami.”

item.title: “Quite honest what I remember? Jimmy Butler was …
item.description: Heat Central: Bobby Portis explains what led to the face off with Spo and Udonis Haslem 💀 “Quite honest what I remember? Jimmy Butler was kicking our…. I can’t even say the bad word…. I remember coming here game 4 up 16 with 9 minutes left… then this guy just goes into straight flow state… Game 5 comes and I’m trying to fire my team up. Right after the timeout UD came up to me and got in my face which is kind of funny I’m here now.”

item.title: Warriors behind Miami, Cleveland, Philadelphia in LeBron James sweepstakes
item.description: Heat Central: “Heat, complete contender the moment he gets there. The Warriors would need a little work still. The big thing with the Warriors was packaging LeBron James with another star player…. There’s been no real traction on the Warriors and AD… They are to my knowledge behind other teams such as a Miami, CLE, PHI.” Shams also mentioned the Sixers didn’t even get on his list until they got Jaylen Brown.

item.title: LeBron James decision might not come this week
item.description: Heat Central: Shams on LeBron James: “I don’t know if I’m holding my breath for an announcement this week…. LeBron has had multiple owners/GMs about 3-5 that have sent voice memos to him via Rich Paul… When I really dig more on the situation the focus is really on the top contending teams for this being the Cavaliers, the Heat, and the 76ers.”

item.title: Draymond's switching to No. 5 if LeBron goes to the …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Draymond's switching to No. 5 if LeBron goes to the Warriors

item.title: “I couldn’t make a shot,” Curry said via text message …
item.description: Steph Curry remembers two things about his only NBA summer-league season as a rookie 17 years ago. First, he recalled being lousy. The ballyhooed No. 7 pick of the 2009 NBA Draft introduced himself to the Golden State Warriors by shooting 32.5 percent from the field over five lackluster games. “I couldn’t make a shot,” Curry said via text message on Wednesday.

item.title: “He had the gym absolutely buzzing,” Curry said. “I’m …
item.description: His second and more vivid memory is that another Warriors guard did just fine. On July 16, Anthony Morrow, an undrafted shooting guard from Georgia Tech, set UNLV’s Cox Pavilion ablaze with what remains one of the greatest — and most audacious — individual performances in the 22-year history of the Las Vegas tournament. It was on this date that Morrow scored 47 points in a little over 36 minutes played against the New Orleans Hornets for a mark that has rarely been threatened at the annual summe

item.title: “As a shooter, you get into that groove. I got in the …
item.description: Morrow, now 40, remembers channeling his inner Ray Allen — “one of my favorite players ever” — and launched shots without apology or hesitation. He made 18 of 26 from the field, including 7 of 9 from 3-point range. Curry, in comparison, chipped in nine points in a supporting role. “As a shooter, you get into that groove. I got in the groove that day, and it really kind of set the tone for the rest of my career,” Morrow said. “Like, it really etched me in stone.”

item.title: “Sure, people say, ‘Well, summer league doesn’t mean …
item.description: Keith Smart, who served as the Warriors’ summer-league coach in 2009, said he uses Morrow’s story as a teaching point to this day. “Sure, people say, ‘Well, summer league doesn’t mean anything.’ Oh, no, no. It means a lot,” Smart told The Athletic by phone this week. “When a guy like Morrow gets his shots, that changes his career. At Georgia Tech, everybody knew he could shoot it, OK? But they didn’t know how he could shoot it in the pros. “I share that with a lot of kids. The message is, ‘If yo

item.title: Jeremy Lin: Warriors won’t be contenders even if they land LeBron James
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jeremy Lin on if the Warriors will be title contenders IF they get LeBron James: 'That's not a championship contending team, even with LeBron. I don't see them beating OKC, I don't see them beating San Antonio unless there is some serious injuries. I think they need some youth, they some energy, they need some burst, they need multiple guys who are young and can bring a lot of energy.'

item.title: The Warriors and Lakers, I'm told, are likely to meet …
item.description: One bit of scheduling news that we do have: The Warriors and Lakers, I'm told, are likely to meet in Las Vegas in October in a preseason game … which would result in an early reunion for LeBron with the purple and gold IFFFF he decides to sign with Golden State.

item.title: Mario Chalmers on LeBron James next destination: 'I think it's between Miami and Philly'
item.description: LeBron is part of this Fanatics event, so maybe he will share a decision soon. Where do you think he plays? Mario Chalmers: (laughs). “It’s hard to say. But I think it’s between Miami and Philly. Those are my two top choices.” Why those two? Chalmers: “I feel like both of those teams are in a good win-now situation. Miami has still added a couple of pieces. I feel like Golden State is a good option. But I just don’t see him staying in the West right now with the East wide open. That’s the only t

item.title: “I feel like both of those teams are in a good win-now …
item.description: Why those two? Chalmers: “I feel like both of those teams are in a good win-now situation. Miami has still added a couple of pieces. I feel like Golden State is a good option. But I just don’t see him staying in the West right now with the East wide open. That’s the only thing.”

item.title: With Miami, I see LeBron being a starting point guard …
item.description: If he goes to Miami or Philly, what do you make of the dynamic and fit with each team? Chalmers: “With Philly, I see him fitting perfectly with Jaylen Brown, [Tyrese] Maxey, and [Joel] Embiid. You have a ‘Big 4’ there. ‘Bron can play in that 3 or 4 position and also take Maxey off the ball. That will help him grow and become a better scorer, better player, and better playmaker. With Miami, I see LeBron being a starting point guard with Bam and Giannis right there on the side. They just got to ge

item.title: “I started off thinking it was Golden State. Then I …
item.description: Dru Star: “I started off thinking it was Golden State. Then I moved on to Cleveland. But then somebody talked me into Miami. And I go, ‘Oh yeah, I guess I could see that you know.’ From a basketball fit the Heat's not a bad fit [for LeBron]. Philly I don't get that at all.” @ramonashelburne

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg: Yeah, I've talked to Steph and …
item.description: Tomer Azarly: Not a lot of guys get to say they come in and play with Stephen Curry, Draymond Green, under head coach Steve Kerr. I'm wondering, has that sunk in for you? Have you let that sink in? What's that been like? Yaxel Lendeborg: Yeah, I've talked to Steph and Draymond a couple of times, just about how to navigate the rookie year. What are the things that I should do to try to stay in the league as long as they did. And just getting the actual feedback, actually talking to them, getting
